tool_args.label: S79 on Hylan Boulevard +selectbus service Progress Report

payload.title: S79 on Hylan Boulevard +selectbus service Progress Report

tool_args.label: MTA New York City Transit (NYCT)
New York City Department of Transportation (NYCDOT)

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

tool_args.label: S79 Select Bus Service

payload.description: Select Bus Service along the S79 route, primarily along Hylan Boulevard, implemented in September 2012 by MTA New York City Transit and the New York City Department of Transportation

tool_args.label: Hylan Boulevard

payload.description: One of the most important transportation corridors on Staten Island, serving as the primary route along Staten Island's east and south shores. Carries local bus services connecting to the Staten Island Ferry and to Brooklyn, and express bus services to Manhattan.

tool_args.label: Richmond Avenue

payload.description: Segment of the S79 bus route traveling to and from the Staten Island Mall, with four travel lanes in each direction plus a wide center median. Offset bus lanes installed from Platinum Avenue to just south of Forest Hill Road and approaching Hylan Boulevard.

tool_args.label: S79 Select Bus Service

payload.description: Second busiest bus route on Staten Island, providing service between the Staten Island Mall and the 86th Street subway station in Bay Ridge, Brooklyn, via Hylan Boulevard and Richmond Avenue.

tool_args.label: S78 local bus

payload.description: Local bus route along Hylan Boulevard providing frequent service.

tool_args.label: S59 local bus

payload.description: Local bus route along Richmond Avenue providing frequent service.

tool_args.label: S79 SBS Launch

payload.description: S79 Select Bus Service launched on Hylan Boulevard corridor

tool_args.label: Open House #1 - December 2010

payload.description: First open house introducing the S79 SBS project

tool_args.label: Open House #2 - June 2011

payload.description: Second open house discussing various design options

tool_args.label: Open House #3 - September 2011

payload.description: Third open house presenting overall design and bus service plan

tool_args.label: Open House #4 - March 2012

payload.description: Fourth open house providing details on preferred service plan and roadway designs

tool_args.label: Curbside bus lanes on Hylan Boulevard

payload.locations: Hylan Boulevard between Lincoln Avenue and Steuben Street
payload.location_text: Hylan Boulevard between Lincoln Avenue and Steuben Street
payload.description: Curbside bus lanes on Hylan Boulevard, terra cotta red, in effect from 6:00-9:00 am northbound and 3:00-7:00 pm southbound

locations_normalized.raw_text: Hylan Boulevard between Lincoln Avenue and Steuben Street

normalized_location.raw_text: Hylan Boulevard between Lincoln Avenue and Steuben Street

tool_args.label: Offset bus lanes on Richmond Avenue

payload.locations: Richmond Avenue near the Staten Island Mall and southbound Richmond Avenue approaching Hylan Boulevard
payload.location_text: Richmond Avenue near the Staten Island Mall and southbound Richmond Avenue approaching Hylan Boulevard
payload.description: Offset bus lanes located in the second lane from the right curb, in effect at all times of the day, preserves curbside parking or loading activity

locations_normalized.raw_text: Richmond Avenue near the Staten Island Mall and southbound Richmond Avenue approaching Hylan Boulevard

normalized_location.raw_text: Richmond Avenue near the Staten Island Mall and southbound Richmond Avenue approaching Hylan Boulevard

tool_args.label: New sidewalks and ADA ramps

payload.location_text: 32 locations along the Hylan Boulevard corridor
payload.description: Upgraded sidewalks at 32 locations and ADA accessible ramps with tactile warning strips installed near bus stops

normalized_location.raw_text: 32 locations along the Hylan Boulevard corridor

tool_args.label: Pedestrian safety islands

payload.location_text: Five signalized intersections on Hylan Boulevard where medians were wide enough
payload.description: Pedestrian safety islands constructed at five signalized intersections to provide refuge for pedestrians unable to finish crossing during the Walk phase

normalized_location.raw_text: Five signalized intersections on Hylan Boulevard where medians were wide enough

tool_args.label: Streamlined travel path rerouting

payload.description: S79 service streamlined at three locations - two near the Staten Island Mall and northbound route revised to avoid double loop through the Eltingville Transit Center, saving approximately five to nine minutes in travel time in each direction

tool_args.label: Transit Signal Priority (planned)

payload.description: Active transit signal priority technology to allow buses to communicate with traffic signals to shorten red or extend green signals

tool_args.label: BusTime real-time information system

payload.description: BusTime system providing real time bus information via web and SMS text-based on-demand bus location information, launched in Staten Island in September 2012

tool_args.label: Left turn bays and signal timing upgrades

payload.description: Left turn bays added at two intersections, street geometry adjusted, flush medians used to narrow travel lanes, and traffic signal timing better coordinated along the corridor

tool_args.label: Bus lane enforcement cameras

payload.description: Bus lane enforcement cameras installed beginning summer 2013 to keep bus lanes clear during operating hours

warnings: treatment_component.enforcement_authority is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Authority names refer to agency/entity records. Suggested relation: enforced_by.

tool_args.label: Reduced S79 stations from 80 to 18

payload.description: S79 SBS operates with fewer, more widely-spaced stations; reduced from 80 stops to 18 stops, transforming to limited-stop bus service

tool_args.label: S79 bus speed improvement

payload.raw_value_text: 13-19% improvement in bus speeds on the S79

warnings: metric_claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids on metric payloads refer to route entities and should become explicit relations when supported. Suggested relation: route has_metric metric_claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

tool_args.label: S79 is Phase 1 BRT project citywide

payload.claim_text: The study identified candidate corridors for BRT leading to the selection of five 'Phase 1' projects citywide, which were branded as Select Bus Service (SBS)
payload.description: S79 Hylan Boulevard is one of the Phase 1 SBS routes, implemented in 2012 following Bx12 (2008), M15 (2010), and M34 (2011)

tool_args.label: No off-board fare collection on this corridor

payload.description: S79 SBS on Hylan Boulevard did not use off-board fare collection, unlike some other SBS implementations
